title: Example: Dynamically Change HTML Attributes in ProcScript
breadcrumbs: Example: Dynamically Change HTML Attributes in ProcScript
text:
Example: Dynamically Change HTML Attributes in ProcScript You can use the AttributesOnly widget in dynamic server pages to manipulate HTML in ProcScript. In this example, you create a simple dynamic server page that enables the user to press a button to completely change the look and feel of the resulting web page. Create a simple DSP with a non-database entity and four fields. In the Define Structure worksheet, use the indicated templates to create the fields, and set the properties as specified: Template Field Name Initial Value AttributesOnly Attributes StaticText (secure) MyData This is data to display CommandButton (secure) SetFormat Set Format CommandButton (secure) ClearFormat Clear Format In the Write Script worksheet, program the detail triggers of these buttons to change the field properties of the ATTRIBUTES field. trigger detail ; of SetFormat field public web $fieldproperties(ATTRIBUTES)="HTML:CLASS=format1" end; detail Copy trigger detail ; of ClearFormat field public web $fieldproperties(ATTRIBUTES)="HTML:CLASS=format0" end; Copy In the Design Layout worksheet, create the HTML layout. Copy the ATTRIBUTES, SETFORMAT, and CLEARFORMAT fields into the layout. For example, choose Copy special >As unformatted HTML . Copy the MYDATA field and copy it inside the element bound to the ATTRIBUTES field. Optionally, add some
elements to provide some line spacing. Define the style classes in a CSS style sheet used by the DSP, or inline in the element. For example: Copy Compile and test the DSP. At runtime, when the user clicks on the SetFormat button, all the fields inside the
tags of the Attributes fields are reformatted according to the format1 style. Figure 1. Formatting Cleared (CSS: format0) Figure 2. Formatting Set (CSS: format1) Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
